definitions.hemorrhagic anovulatory follicle: An ovarian follicle that fails to ovulate and undergoes intrafollicular hemorrhage, resulting in the antral cavity becoming filled with blood rather than follicular fluid, without rupture or formation of a corpus luteum.

unresolvable.label: hemorrhagic anovulatory follicle
unresolvable.reason: Input parent_id UBERON:0001424 (ulna) is incorrect for this ovarian anatomy term. Correct parent resolved to UBERON:0001305 (ovarian follicle) via OLS4 search and anatomical knowledge. The term has no existing UBERON equivalent and no dedicated Wikipedia article. The two def_xref DOIs (Gougeon 1986 Hum Reprod; Richards et al 2018 Endocr Rev) are background references for follicle biology and theca cell differentiation, not HAF-specific; the ASCTB-TEMP IRI is the primary xref for the HRA source.
unresolvable.suggestion: Assign is_a UBERON:0001305 (ovarian follicle) and part_of UBERON:0000992 (ovary). Consider also relating to UBERON:0007180 (atretic follicle of ovary) via a sibling relationship note, as HAF differs from atresia by featuring intrafollicular hemorrhage rather than granulosa cell apoptosis.
